Welcome to our Crafting & Supply

The magic happens at our crafting facilities, where we combine centuries-old crafting traditions and high-quality materials with modern production techniques. You will be warmly greeted by over 13,000 craftspeople who are excellent at crafting and supplying the world’s largest jewelry brand with our craftsmanship. 

Join a team that is as diverse and inclusive as our jewellery. We believe in and value creative thinking and ensure that there are equitable opportunities for all. Foster growth and craft opportunities through our learning and development programmes. At Pandora, no effort or contribution goes unnoticed. We offer a wide range of opportunities and benefits to ensure that our employees feel valued, supported, and cared for. Transportation services are also available to drop you at the other community areas. 

About Pandora

Pandora is the world's largest jewellery brand, specialising in the design, crafting and marketing of accessible luxury jewellery made from high-quality materials. Each piece is created to inspire self-expression, allowing people to share their stories and passions through meaningful jewellery. Pandora jewellery is sold in more than 100 countries through 6,800 points of sale, including more than 2,700 concept stores. Headquartered in Copenhagen, Denmark, Pandora employs 37,000 people worldwide and crafts its jewellery using only recycled silver and gold. Pandora is committed to leadership in sustainability and has set out to halve greenhouse gas emissions across its value chain by 2030. Pandora is listed on the Nasdaq Copenhagen stock exchange and generated revenue of DKK 31.7 billion (EUR 4.2 billion) in 2024.

About Pandora Production Thailand

Pandora started crafting its jewellery in Thailand in 1989, where our founders fell in love with the delicate craftsmanship of the Thai people. To date, there are two crafting facilities in Thailand, Bangkok, and Lamphun, and we employ more than 13,000 skilled craftspeople. By the end of 2024, there are 113 million pieces sold globally, all of which were hand-finished here in Thailand by our skilled craftspeople using high-quality raw materials and sustainable methods.

Job Overview

We'll be honest with you — this is one of the most important and highest-expectation roles at Gofive. We hold this seat to an extraordinary standard. But here's what's also true: you don't need to have done this job before to be the right person for it.

What we're really looking for is someone who can't stop asking why. Someone who studies products in their free time not because they have to, but because they genuinely can't help it. Someone who walks into a product, finds ten things that could be better, and immediately starts thinking about how. If you're the kind of person who obsesses over customer experiences, reads product teardowns for fun, and loses sleep over a clunky user flow — we want to meet you.

At Gofive, we're a product-led, AI-first tech company that has been transforming how over 5,000 businesses across Thailand work — from HR and payroll to sales CRM, omnichannel chat, and eTax. We've earned national recognition for our product quality. And we're just getting started.

This role sits at the absolute center of what we build and why. You'll work directly alongside our COO and CEO. You'll talk to stakeholders the morning and turn what you heard into something real by afternoon. You'll build prototypes, rally developers, dig into data, and argue passionately for your users.

You'll cover more ground here in one year than most people do in three. It will be a relentless, exhilarating, occasionally humbling ride — and if that sentence excites you more than it scares you, this role was written for you.

Responsibilities

Pursue the product relentlessly. Spend real time studying our products, our competitors, and the market. Don't wait to be told what's broken or what's missing — go find it. Develop a point of view, back it up, and fight for it.

Live inside the customer's world. Talk to customers constantly. Sit in on sales calls. Read support tickets. Run interviews. Understand not just what users say they want, but what they actually need — and bring those insights back sharp enough to change what we build.

Own the product vision end-to-end. You're not executing someone else's ideas. You research, synthesize, propose, debate, and decide. You take full ownership of outcomes — not just outputs.

Build prototypes yourself. Don't wait for someone else to make your idea tangible. Sketch it, wireframe it, prototype it — fast and good enough to test. Move ideas out of your head and into something people can react to.

Drive AI-first thinking. Every product decision carries the question: how does AI make this genuinely better for our users? Not as a feature to check off — as a real force multiplier.

Get everyone on board. Bring developers, designers, sales, customer success, and leadership into the vision. Explain the why behind every decision with clarity and conviction. Make people believe in what you're building.

Iterate without ego. Ship. Learn. Throw away what isn't working. Do it again, better. You treat every release as a conversation with your users, not a finish line.

Obsess over every detail. The micro-copy. The three-click flow that should be one. The first-time user experience. Nothing is too small if it shapes how a customer feels. We have an eye for every detail at Gofive — and so will you.

Do whatever it takes. There is no "that's not my job" here. If the product needs it, you find a way. You pick up what's dropped, you fill the gaps, you go the extra mile without being asked. The product's success is your success — and you feel that in your bones.

Qualifications

We care far less about your resume than about how you think and how you operate. This role is open to candidates from any background — product, design, engineering, business, customer success, or none of the above — as long as you bring the right fundamentals.

What we're looking for:

Relentless curiosity — you study products, markets, and customers not because someone asked you to, but because you genuinely can't stop

Self-driven and hungry — you don't need to be told what to do next; you see what needs doing and you go do it

Extreme ownership — you treat every outcome as yours, even when it's hard, even when it's inconvenient

Comfort with ambiguity — you can operate in a fast-moving environment where the path isn't always clear and that energizes you, not paralyzes you

A natural communicator — you can make complex ideas simple and bring a room full of different stakeholders to the same page

Data curiosity — you ask questions of numbers, not just accept them; you dig, hypothesize, and let evidence challenge your assumptions

Empathy for users — you feel genuinely bothered when something is hard to use, and genuinely satisfied when you make it better

Nice to have — but never required:

Full_Description:
Scope Overview

The Digital Architect (AEC) serves as a principal strategic advisor to senior management, providing enterprise-level governance and steering the alignment of business objectives with technological investments across subsidiaries.

This role establishes regional architecture standards and frameworks, overseeing the convergence of enterprise strategy, process optimization, and scalable technology. Acting as the authority on business capability models and technical solution reviews, the Architect perform M&A Due Diligence, post-deal integration strategies, on top of cross-border architectural compliance, ensuring all regional systems and acquired entities natively integrate with MUFG and Krungsri principles.




What you'll do

Business Architecture Governance & Strategy

Strategic Oversight: Provide executive guidance to senior stakeholders on translating regional business strategy into standardized capability models and long-term architectural roadmaps.

Artifact Governance: Govern and review business architecture artifacts (e.g., capability maps, value streams) to ensure regional alignment with overarching enterprise standards.

Process Consultation: Consult on the definition of target operating models and organizational processes, ensuring regional transformation initiatives adhere to the broader enterprise vision.

Requirement Validation: Review and validate complex business requirements and user journeys to guarantee architectural traceability to strategic business objectives.

Readiness Steering: Steer organizational readiness evaluations and advise regional leadership on the architectural implications, risks, and synergies of major business shifts.

Regional Business Capability Governance: Own and govern regional business capability models to ensure consistency and strategic alignment across AEC entities.

Strategy‑to‑Capability Alignment: Translate business strategies into clear target‑state capability roadmaps that guide transformation and integration priorities.

Capability‑Based Investment & Prioritization: Ensure investments are driven by business capabilities, with clear traceability from strategic objectives to enabling solutions.

Operating Model & Value Stream Alignment: Align target operating models, value streams, and end‑to‑end processes with the future‑state capability architecture.

Capability Readiness & Maturity Oversight: Assess capability readiness, maturity, and risk to support executive decision‑making and M&A integration.

 

Technical Architecture Review & Assurance

Design Approval: Evaluate and approve scalable, high-availability technical designs proposed by regional delivery teams, ensuring adherence to approved enterprise technology stacks and modern architecture patterns.

Integration Governance: Govern integration architectures across the AEC region, mandating robust API management, API gateway strategies, and secure service-to-service communication standards.

Resiliency Consulting: Consult on and review cloud-native deployment topologies and system observability frameworks, guaranteeing cross-border application resilience and performance transparency.

Security Enforcement: Enforce enterprise security, centralized secret management, and data sovereignty compliance policies for all regional applications and cross-border data flows.

Standard Leverage: Identify opportunities to leverage standard design / solution between subsidiaries and KS.

 

Mergers & Acquisitions (M&A) and Regional Integration Advisory

Due Diligence (DD) Oversight: Review scope and architecture assessments for prospective targets. Critically evaluate target technology stacks, digital channels, API readiness, and innovation capabilities against enterprise standards.

M&A Advisory: Provide executive debriefs and definitive architectural rulings in DD reports, advising the M&A steering committee on technical debt, integration risks, and technological capabilities. Maintain and optimize the standard frameworks and templates for digital assessments.

Integration Governance (Post-Deal): Define the architectural blueprints and technical mandates for integrating newly acquired entities into the KS Group ecosystem.

Impact Review: Review and validate integration impact assessments provided by delivery teams, ensuring that proposed development efforts are structurally sound and align with enterprise integration patterns.

Post-Deal Advisory (BAU): Serve as the principal architectural consultant for KS partners and MUFG entities regarding digital solutions, actively championing cross-group technology reuse, platform sharing, and continuous architectural improvement.




Apply now if you have these advantages

Bachelor’s or Master’s degree (preferred) in Business Administration (MBA), Information System, Information Technology, Computer Science, Finance or related field.

7+ years of solid, demonstrable experience in the financial services sector or financial services consulting sector, with a focus on enterprise-scale environments.

3+ years of experience operating in an enterprise architecture, governance, or strategic advisory role, bridging both Business Architecture and Technical/Solution Architecture domains.

Enterprise & Business Architecture: Knowledge of architecture frameworks (e.g., TOGAF, BIAN) and banking domain standards (e.g., ISO-20022, Open Banking).

Integration & API Strategy: Understanding of API-led connectivity, enterprise API Gateways, connection pool management, and microservices communication patterns.

Cloud-Native & Infrastructure: Knowledge of container orchestration platforms, network configurations, and hybrid-cloud deployment topologies.

Enterprise Security & Compliance: Understanding of Zero-Trust architecture, centralized secrets management, identity access management (IAM), and cross-border data privacy regulations.

Observability & Performance Governance: Knowledge of enterprise application performance monitoring (APM) and full-stack observability platforms to establish standards for system health, telemetry, and issue resolution across regional entities.

Architectural Governance & Review: Ability to evaluate, critique, and approve technical designs and business requirement documents submitted by regional delivery teams or vendors.

Technical Assessment: Skill in reviewing multi-disciplinary tech stacks to assess whether a target entity's architecture is viable for enterprise integration.

Strategic Translation: Capability to translate business expansion strategies into technology roadmaps, capability models, and technical integration mandates.

Due Diligence Execution: Ability to assess a target company's digital maturity, identify security vulnerabilities, evaluate technical debt, and synthesize findings into advisory reports.

Cross-Cultural Stakeholder Management: Facilitation, negotiation, and influence skills required to drive architectural consensus across regional teams, Krungsri subsidiaries, and MUFG partners.

Analytical Problem Solving: Analytical skills to map dependencies between business processes and legacy IT systems during post-deal integrations.

Good in English skill both written and speaking is a MUST.

Must be able to undertake short-term overseas assignments (approximately 3–6 months) to support critical programs or platform replacement initiatives at subsidiary locations.

About LEARN Corporation

We use technology to make learning accessible for all ages, with a dream to serve as a private 'ministry of education' that genuinely supports learners nationwide.

But to transform education at a national scale, our internal engine must operate at the speed of tomorrow. We cannot build the future of learning on legacy workflows. This is where your true impact begins. By rewiring our operations with AI, you are doing much more than eliminating bottlenecks—you are creating a massive multiplier effect. Every hour of manual work you automate is an hour our teams redirect toward innovating education, expanding our reach, and fundamentally helping to make Thailand's future better and better.

About This Role

As a core member of the CEO Office’s AI Special Team, your mission is to elevate the organization's systemic capabilities by redesigning workflows with AI to maximize productivity and quality. Moving beyond a traditional advisory role, you will be embedded directly into specific business units or corporate functions. As the technical co-lead of a dedicated workstream, you will work hand-in-hand with Domain Experts to resolve bottlenecks, unlock new possibilities, and drive measurable transformation from the ground up. You are expected to seamlessly map AI solutions to business workflows—ranging from suggesting GenAI prompts for immediate quick wins, to taking a hands-on approach to independently build small-to-medium automations and POCs, to ultimately scoping larger, complex initiatives to collaborate with our core development teams.

What You'll Drive

1. AI Solution Mapping & Workflow Redesign

Partner with business unit / corporate function teams and domain experts to deep-dive into current workflows, identifying inefficiencies, systemic bottlenecks, and potential areas where AI can help drive new capabilities.

Evaluate and select the most appropriate AI tools, models, or automation platforms to solve specific business pain points effectively.

2. Rapid Prototyping & Proof of Concept (POC)

Act with a 'Maker Mindset' to independently build, test, and deploy small-to-medium automations and AI prototypes (using GenAI, prompt engineering, low-code/no-code platforms, or basic scripting).

Execute fast-paced POCs with pilot teams to validate technical feasibility, user acceptance, and business ROI before company-wide scaling.

Strategically scope larger, more complex initiatives that require enterprise-grade development, acting as the key liaison to collaborate with core IT/development teams.

Take shared accountability for the project's success, ensuring that the implemented technology actually delivers the promised productivity and improvement in quality.

3. Change Facilitation & Tech Unblocking

Serve as the bridge between cutting-edge technology and non-technical staff, translating complex AI concepts into practical, everyday language.

Coach and guide frontline employees during the adoption phase to minimize resistance and ensure seamless integration of AI into their daily routines.

Actively resolve technical roadblocks (e.g., data security concerns, IT policy constraints, system integrations) to keep the task force moving at speed.

4. AI Culture & Capability Building

Champion AI adoption across LEARN Corp by driving upskilling and reskilling initiatives to empower our employees with AI capabilities.

Foster and boost an internal AI community, leading related activities and workshops to cultivate a future-ready, AI-driven culture within the organization.

What You’ll Bring

1. AI Fluency & Tech-Savviness

Great knowledge of the current AI landscape, including Generative AI (LLMs), AI Agents, and Copilot / Cowork ecosystems.

A clear understanding of both the capabilities and limitations/risks of AI technologies, ensuring safe and compliant implementations (e.g., data privacy awareness).

2. Maker Mindset & Prototyping Skills

Great Prompt Engineering skills.

Proficiency in Low-code/No-code automation tools or basic programming (e.g., Python, API integrations) to build working prototypes independently.

3. Strong Business Acumen & Soft Skills

Solution-oriented mindset: Highly focused on solving problems and delivering results, measuring technology impact in terms of business metrics (ROI, Cost-saving, Speed, Quality).

Empathy and communication skills; capable of listening to operational pain points without technical bias, and collaborating seamlessly with cross-functional teams.

Thrives in a fast-paced, ambiguous environment and possesses a 'skin in the game' mentality—treating the project's success or failure as their own.

Education & Experience

3-5 years of proven experience in digital transformation, tech consulting, process optimization, or Automation implementation.

Note: Highly capable new graduates with a strong portfolio or proven experience in real-world AI/Automation projects are also warmly welcome for consideration.

Our Ascend vision is to create life opportunities with innovative digital services. Operating in Southeast Asia, we are able to help one of the world’s largest populations of underbanked, the people from some of the poorest provinces who are disregarded by traditional banks. In 2018, we served over 30 million customers in 6 countries (Thailand, Cambodia, Myanmar, Vietnam, Indonesia, Philippines), and processed over 4.5 billion USD. This makes us by far the largest fintech company in SE Asia and growing quickly.

Job Title: Tester Engineer

Company: Sony Device Technology (Thailand) Co., Ltd. (SDT)

Location: Bang Kadi Industrial Park, Pathum Thani

Employment Type: Full-time




About Sony Device Technology (Thailand)

Sony Device Technology (Thailand) Co., Ltd. (SDT) serves as a core production and testing facility for Sony’s semiconductor business. Our facility in Bang Kadi Industrial Park specializes in the assembly and testing of Image Sensors and microscale semiconductor devices integrated into automotive safety and imaging systems.




Job Summary

As a Tester Engineer, you are responsible for maintaining and optimizing the high-precision semiconductor testing environment. Your role involves managing the technical lifecycle of test hardware, performing deep-dive circuit analysis to resolve failures, and utilizing data automation to drive yield improvements. You will ensure all testing processes comply with automotive-grade quality standards and support seamless product transitions from NPI to mass production.




Key Responsibilities

Manage the end-to-end technical setup, calibration, and hands-on maintenance of Automatic Test Equipment (ATE), including load boards, probers, and specialized fixtures. Perform advanced circuit analysis at the component level to diagnose complex hardware failures and electrical signal integrity issues. Utilize multimeters, oscilloscopes, and logic analyzers to execute hardware repairs and ensure 100% equipment readiness for New Product Introduction (NPI) and sustained mass production.

Analyze large-scale production datasets to identify yield detractors, cycle time bottlenecks, and manufacturing inefficiencies. Develop and implement Excel Macros (VBA) and custom automation scripts to streamline data processing, validate test result integrity, and automate technical reporting. Lead Root Cause Analysis (RCA) investigations into non-conforming (NG) defects using 8D or Why-Why methodologies to deploy permanent technical solutions and improve overall test stability.

Standardize technical documentation, including Operating Procedures (SOPs) and IC qualification protocols, ensuring strict adherence to automotive quality standards (IATF 16949). Partner with Design, Production, and Quality teams to evaluate "Design for Testability" (DFT) based on circuit performance data. Manage test correlation activities across multiple equipment platforms to ensure consistent accuracy and reliability across all production lots.




Qualifications & Competencies

Minimum Qualifications:

Bachelor’s Degree in Electronics Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical discipline.

0 – 3 years of experience in test engineering, equipment maintenance, or electronics manufacturing.

Demonstrated ability in circuit analysis and hardware diagnostics; skilled in using Multimeters, Oscilloscopes, and Logic Analyzers for troubleshooting.

High proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including the ability to develop and debug Macros and VBA scripts for data automation and analysis.

Job_Title: Solution Sales Executive- SAP Finance and Spend Management - Thailand
Full_Description:
We help the world run betterAt SAP, we keep it simple: you bring your best to us, and we'll bring out the best in you. We're builders touching over 20 industries and 80% of global commerce, and we need your unique talents to help shape what's next. The work is challenging – but it matters. You'll find a place where you can be yourself, prioritize your wellbeing, and truly belong. What's in it for you? Constant learning, skill growth, great benefits, and a team that wants you to grow and succeed. 

Role Overview

The Solution Account Lead (SAL) is a strategic leader who combines deep SAP expertise, business acumen, and end-to-end solution area domain knowledge to drive LoB cloud revenue, customer success, accelerate adoption of innovation (especially AI), and position the organization as a market leader. The SAL shapes transformative solutions and delivers measurable value to customers through thought leadership, innovation, and strategic execution—supporting both Finance and Spend Management and the overall “One SAP” strategy.

Key Responsibilities


Account Ownership & Strategy: Serve as the SAP Finance and Spend Management owner for assigned accounts, developing and executing strategic account plans that align with customer goals, business objectives and broader account plans by account team.

Drive End-to-End Customer Value Journey with Domain Expertise: Provide solution area domain expertise and thought leadership to understand and address high-priority business challenges. Lead end-to-end process mapping and the customer value journey, owning the transformation roadmap for SAP Finance and Spend Management.  

Pipeline & Opportunity Management: Identify and develop new business opportunities within existing accounts, contributing to pipeline growth and revenue targets.

Product Success & Innovation: Drive go-to-market for new products, engage early with customers, validate solutions, and influence the product roadmap. Lead AI and innovation initiatives (e.g., BDC, Knowledge Graphs, scalable PoCs, Joule agents).

Enablement, Demos & Prototypes: Support solution advisors to ensure demo system readiness and manage enablement programs. Collaborate with Demo & Learning teams to provide updated assets and trial environments as part of scalable enablement programs as well as customized demos, POCs and prototypes with customer specific data.

Value Proposition & Executive Engagement: Collaborate with value advisors to create compelling narratives articulating ROI, value leakage and competitive advantages. Conduct strategic discovery, run value leakage, AOTP workshops and deliver persuasive pitches with ways of solving distinct customer business/buying-center challenges to accelerate executive buy-in and drive demand independently of RFPs.

Commercial Negotiations: Navigate complex pricing and contractual discussions, balancing client expectations with organizational profitability and cloud revenue growth.

Adoption & Consumption: Support Customer Success Management (CSM) communities, secure references, and manage escalations. Collaborate with CS and CS&D adoption teams to ensure successful delivery of solutions and services, monitoring outcomes and driving continuous improvement to maximize customer value.

Customer Success & Field Impact: Own SAP Finance and Spend Management deal cycles, renewals, enablement, and executive engagement. Elevate customer discussions to prioritize investment and drive measurable outcomes.

Relationship Building & Governance: Drive C-suite engagements and Buying Center Alignment by fostering long-term high-value relationships and convert executives into advocates and brand ambassadors. Conduct Quarterly Business Reviews (QBRs) with customers and internal stakeholders focused on solution adoption, innovation, risks and opportunities.

Ecosystem & Partner Engagement: Own relationships with strategic consulting partners (e.g., McKinsey, Bain, Accenture, Deloitte, PwC, EY) to co-innovate, develop joint go-to-market strategies, and shape disruptive solutions. Maintain direct, high-quality relationships with partner account leads.

Collaboration & Orchestration: Align closely with Sales, Product, and Marketing to ensure SAL impact is fully integrated into the go-to-market engine with tailored GTM approach. Drive joint accountability and coordination across internal stakeholders for maximum market impact.

Competitive & Industry Expertise: Maintain deep technical and functional knowledge across SAP solutions. Stay at the forefront of emerging technologies and competitive trends to craft differentiated solutions that win in the market.

Qualifications & Competencies

Bachelor’s degree in Business, Marketing, Information Technology, or related field.
Management consulting type profile with 10-15 Years Industry or Practitioner experience; Executive relationship building skills with proven C-suite influence.  
B2B enterprise experience with multi-stakeholder SaaS cycles, plus top-tier consulting and deep industry expertise.
Proven experience in account management, solution sales, or customer success roles.
Strong understanding of solution sales, customer value realization, and account planning methodologies; Expansion selling track record (account growth).
Deep SAP and domain expertise, with strong understanding of AI and innovation trends.
Maps value levers and tell a quantified ROI storytelling and compelling business case creation
Strategic thinking, business acumen, relationship building and client advocacy skills.
Excellent communication, negotiation, and stakeholder management abilities.
Ability to work collaboratively in a matrixed environment and influence without direct authority.
Analytical mindset with a focus on problem-solving and continuous improvement.
12 plus years’ experience in a quota carrying role 
4 plus years’ domain experience in applicable sub solution area domain either from related industry or consulting (Spend management solutions)
 

Why This Role Matters

The SAL is pivotal in shaping SAP’s market leadership by delivering transformative solutions, driving innovation (especially in AI), and orchestrating value across the customer lifecycle. This role is at the intersection of strategy, technology, and relationship management, ensuring customers realize the full value of their SAP investments while positioning the organization for sustained growth.

Bring out your bestSAP innovations help more than four hundred thousand customers worldwide work together more efficiently and use business insight more effectively. Originally known for leadership in enterprise resource planning (ERP) software, SAP has evolved to become a market leader in end-to-end business application software and related services for database, analytics, intelligent technologies, and experience management. As a cloud company with two hundred million users and more than one hundred thousand employees worldwide, we are purpose-driven and future-focused, with a highly collaborative team ethic and commitment to personal development. Whether connecting global industries, people, or platforms, we help ensure every challenge gets the solution it deserves. At SAP, you can bring out your best.

About the job


As a Network Implementation Engineer, you will be the initial point of our efforts to execute deployment, maintenance, and operations of private data networks worldwide. You will work with Technical Program Managers, Network Engineers, Design and Infrastructure Engineers, Field Engineers within Google, as well as construction and telecommunications vendors and contractors, all to position your team and organization for success.

You will facilitate faster, better, and more efficient, positive outcomes for the business and our customers. Your objective will be to build the world’s most reliable, cost-effective and scalable network to support all of our current and future customers and users globally.

The AI and Infrastructure team is redefining what’s possible. We empower Google customers with breakthrough capabilities and insights by delivering AI and Infrastructure at unparalleled scale, efficiency, reliability and velocity. Our customers include Googlers, Google Cloud customers, and billions of Google users worldwide.

We're the driving team behind Google's groundbreaking innovations, empowering the development of our AI models, delivering unparalleled computing power to global services, and providing the essential platforms that enable developers to build the future. From software to hardware our teams are shaping the future of world-leading hyperscale computing, with key teams working on the development of our TPUs, Vertex AI for Google Cloud, Google Global Networking, Data Center operations, systems research, and much more.

Own network documentation, updating, and maintaining network drawings and plans.

Job Summarize:

As the SAP Project Manager is responsible for leading, planning, and executing the implementation, maintenance, and continuous improvement of the SAP S/4HANA system across the group of companies.

This role oversees end-to-end SAP project delivery, ensuring alignment with business objectives, effective resource management, and successful collaboration between internal resource teams and external vendors.

The incumbent will work closely with SAP functional teams, business key users, end users, and system integrators to ensure seamless operations, system stability, and future scalability of SAP solutions. In addition to core ERP responsibilities, this role plays a pivotal part in driving digital innovation, data management, analytics, and process optimization initiatives within the organization.

Key Responsibilities

1. Lead and Manage SAP S/4HANA project implementation across company business units and the day-to-day operations of the SAP S/4HANA landscape, ensuring optimal performance and availability. Ensuring delivery within scope, time, and budget.

2. Collaborate with cross-functional teams to define and implement SAP S4/HANA strategies and solutions to support the company's business objectives and goals.

3. Collaborate with business stakeholders to identify process improvement opportunities and translate them into system enhancements.

4. Oversee the design, development, and deployment of SAP S/4HANA cloud-based applications and data models.

5. Coordinate and supervise internal teams (SAP functional, business key users, and end users) and external vendors to ensure project milestones are achieved.

6. Provide technical guidance to functional oversight in key SAP modules including FI, CO, SD, MM, PP, PM, QM, WM, and ABAP. And support end-users, and collaborate with the IT functional support team to address user issues

7. Support data governance, reporting, and analytics initiatives to enhance business intelligence and decision-making capabilities.

8. Drive team and support change management efforts across functions.

Qualifications

1. Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, Computer Engineer, Business Administration, or related field.

2. Minimum of a full life-cycle SAP implementation as a functional lead or Project lead.

3. At least 10 years of experience in SAP product preferably with exposure to S/4HANA.

4. Strong problem-solving mindset and analytical skills, with the ability to identify and resolve complex issues with focus on business process improvement.

5. Solid background understanding of SAP modules (FI, CO, SD, MM, PP, PM, QM, WM) and technical integration (ABAP).

6. Excellent communication, leadership, and stakeholder management skills.

7. Industry experience in FMCG is an advantage.

Job_Title: Physical Security & ICT Design Engineer (Data Center)
Full_Description:
Role Description:

We are seeking an experienced Physical Security & ICT Design Engineer to join our mission-critical Data Center design team. The ideal candidate will lead end-to-end design and coordination of ICT infrastructure and physical security systems for greenfield and brownfield Data Center projects. This includes structured cabling, telecom/network infrastructure, MMR/CR/MDA design, hyperscale compliance, system integration, and commissioning. The role requires strong knowledge of industry standards (TIA-942, ISO/IEC 11801, ISO/IEC 22237, ISO/IEC 27001, SBD) and collaboration with internal teams, clients, architects, MEP consultants, carriers, and vendors.

Key Responsibilities

ICT System Design & Development

Lead full lifecycle ICT design for Data Centers, covering structured cabling (copper/fiber), MMR/Carrier Rooms, Meet-me Rooms, MDAs, tenant rooms/racks, OOB/OA/Enterprise networks, fiber backbone (ISP/OSP), containment systems (tray/ladder/racks), conduits, and Wi-Fi/IoT planning.

Produce ICT high-level and detailed designs: SLDs, schematics, risers, rack elevations, cable schedules, BoM/BoQ, technical specs, and tender documents.

Design end-to-end telecom infrastructure including fiber entry pathways, redundant routing, and cross-connect architectures.

Ensure compliance with TIA-942, ISO/IEC 11801, ISO/IEC 22237, Uptime Tier Guidelines, and hyperscaler design guides (AWS, Meta, Google, Microsoft).

Physical Security System Design

Lead design of Access Control, Intrusion Detection, CCTV/Video Surveillance, PIDS, mantraps/secure portals, and control room/GSOC layouts.

Select compliant hardware: cameras, VMS, NDAA-compliant devices, biometric/OSDP card readers, NVRs/servers/storage, turnstiles, electric locks, and egress devices.

Conduct security risk assessments, CPTED reviews, and Security by Design evaluations.

Ensure alignment with local codes, DC operator standards, and international security guidelines.

Project Management & Coordination

Coordinate with architects, MEP, hyperscale/tenant teams, carriers/ISPs, ICT/security vendors, GCs, and subcontractors.

Review shop drawings, material submissions, RFIs, and provide technical guidance throughout the lifecycle.

Track schedules, manage deliverables, and prepare reports, presentations, and client-facing documentation.

Commissioning & Testing

Develop commissioning plans and test scripts for structured cabling, fiber/copper testing (Tier 1/Tier 2), security FAT/SAT, and integrated security systems.

Lead/support commissioning: validate installations, conduct inspections, prepare punch lists, acceptance tests, coordinate with carriers/integrators, and complete test reports, as-builts, and handover packages.

Documentation & Compliance

Produce technical designs, method statements, risk assessments, commissioning reports, as-builts, and O&M manuals.

Ensure compliance with local regulations, international ICT/security standards, and client/corporate quality requirements.

Qualifications & Technical Requirements

Degree in Engineering, ICT, Security, Telecommunications, or related field (preferred).

Minimum 3 years’ experience in ICT and security system design/engineering/commissioning for critical infrastructure (Data Centers preferred).

Proven experience designing/integrating:

Access Control Systems

Intrusion Detection Systems

CCTV/Video Surveillance

Perimeter Detection & Monitoring

Control Room Operations

Structured Cabling (Cat6A, OS2, MPO/MTP)

Cross-connect/patching architectures

Redundant fiber routing, cable management, containment

Carrier/ISP fiber delivery methods

Network room environmental requirements (power/cooling/space)

Proficiency with design tools (AutoCAD, Revit/BIM, Visio), Bluebeam, MS Project.

Preferred certifications: RCDD (BICSI) or equivalent, PSP/CPP, Security by Design credentials, CDCP/CDCS/DCDC.

Strong understanding of local security codes, building regulations, ISO 27001, TIA-942, and low-voltage/structured cabling fundamentals.

Knowledge of network basics (Layer 1/2, VLANs, OOB networks) is advantageous.

Experience collaborating with architects, MEP consultants, telecom/ICT teams.

Strong English communication skills and project management proficiency (MS Project, Trello, etc.).

Preferences

Ability to work independently, prioritize tasks, and manage multiple projects.

Excellent attention to detail, problem-solving skills, and stakeholder management.

Strong analytical and proactive approach.

Reliable, professional, and able to maintain confidentiality.

Team player with leadership capabilities.

Able to thrive under tight deadlines in fast-paced environments.

Willing to travel for site visits and inspections.

Previous Data Center ICT/security design and commissioning experience.

Experience with international DC standards (TIA-942, ISO/IEC 27001, Uptime Tier Guidelines).

Job responsibilities:


Support ระบบ SAP ส่วนของ Business Process, Configuration, Functional Spec และ Implementation เพื่อให้สอดคล้องกับกระบวนการทำงานตามนโยบายบริษัท รวมถึงการสื่อสารกับ Abaper ได้เป็นอย่างดี เพื่อให้เกิดประสิทธิภาพสูงสุดกับธุรกิจขององค์กร

นำเสนอแนวทางในการเพิ่มประสิทธิภาพในการทำงานให้ดีขึ้น รวมถึงวิเคราะห์ความต้องการของธุรกิจเกี่ยวกับกระบวนการทำงานของระบบ SAP และ Interface ต่างๆที่เกี่ยวข้อง

แนะนำและเสนอแนะแนวทางการแก้ไขปัญหาที่เกิดขึ้น ให้กับผู้ใช้งานในระบบ SAP เพื่อให้การทำงานเป็นไปอย่างราบรื่นและเกิดประสิทธิภาพสูงสุด

ประสานงาน ระหว่างทุกฝ่าย ที่เกี่ยวข้องกับซอฟต์แวร์ SAP และ ซอฟแวร์ อื่นๆ เพื่อให้เข้าใจเหตุการณ์หรือข้อมูลที่เกิดขึ้นภายในองค์กรได้ถูกต้อง และเกิดประสิทธิภาพสูงสุด

ตรวจสอบและบริหารจัดการ เอกสารต่าง ๆ ที่เกี่ยวข้องกับระบบ SAP และ ซอฟแวร์ อื่นๆ ให้ทันสมัยและถูกต้องอยู่เสมอ

Qualifications:


ปริญญาตรี สาขา วิศวกรรมคอมพิวเตอร์, วิทยาการคอมพิวเตอร์, สาขาเทคโนโลยีสารสนเทศ หรือเทียบเท่า

มีประสบการณ์ทำงานทาง ด้านระบบ ERP SAP หรือ อื่นๆ ที่เกี่ยวข้องไม่ต่ำกว่า 2 ปี

มีความรู้ การทำงานของระบบ ERP SAP อย่างน้อย 1 โมดูล เช่น MM, SD ในหน้าที่ Support และมีความรู้พื้นฐานเกี่ยวกับระบบงาน ERP ในแต่ละโมดูล

สามารถวิเคราห์ความต้องการของผู้ใช้งาน SAP (AS-IS) พร้อมทั้งให้คำปรึกษาออกแบบระบบเพื่อพัฒนา Application สำหรับผู้ใช้งาน (TO-BE) ได้อย่างเหมาะสม

มีความเข้าใจ และสามารถออกแบบกระบวนการทำงานทางธุรกิจ (Business Process), Function, Technical ระบบงาน SAP ได้อย่างมีประสิทธิภาพ

มีทักษะในการทำงานร่วมกับทีมงาน ที่เกี่ยวข้อง ERP และซอฟแวร์ อื่น มาอย่างน้อย 2 ปี

1. ศึกษารวบรวมความต้องการและวิเคราะห์กระบวนการทาง Business ในปัจจุบัน (Current State Analysis)  2. สรุปขอบเขตการทำงานของแต่ละโครงการ (Project Scope) 3. ตรวจสอบและวิเคราะห์รายละเอียดการดำเนินโครงการ ให้เป็นหมวดหมู่ และประสานงานร่วมกับฝ่ายกลยุทธ์และดิจิทัลเทคโนโลยี รวมถึง Business User 4. ประเมินความต้องการของ Business User และนำไปออกแบบเครื่องมือที่เหมาะสมให้สอดคล้องกับความต้องการใช้งาน 5. ควบคุมการทดสอบและติดตามระบบให้เป็นไปตาม Software Development Life Cycle (SDLC) รับผิดชอบหน้าที่ Business Analysis ของโครงการขนาด S-XS 6. ออกแบบ/ปรับแก้ไขกระบวนการทาง Businessใหม่และกำหนด Use case รวมถึงจัดทำ Test case ที่เกี่ยวข้อง (To-Be Process Design) 7. สื่อสารกับผู้ใช้งานให้เข้าใจถึงการเปลี่ยนแปลงที่เกิดขึ้น และฝึกอบรมหน่วยงานที่เกี่ยวข้อง (Change Management & Training) 8. จัดทำและตรวจสอบเนื้อหาความถูกต้องครบถ้วนของข้อมูลในคู่มือการใช้งาน และเอกสารที่เกี่ยวข้องกับการฝึกอบรมผู้ใช้งานก่อนส่งมอบโครงการ 9. แนะนำให้คำปรึกษาแนวทางแก้ไขของปัญหาเชิงธุรกิจที่เกิดจากการใช้งานระบบ (Hyper Case)

Company Profile

Award-winning executive recruitment company, Monroe Consulting Group, is recruiting on behalf of a leading IT services and system integration provider with a strong and growing presence in Thailand. Our respected client specializes in delivering end-to-end digital transformation solutions, supporting enterprise clients across infrastructure, enterprise applications, and industrial technology platforms


Job Summary

The IT Infrastructure Manager will be responsible for leading the design, implementation, and management of enterprise IT infrastructure solutions, supporting business growth and client delivery across Thailand. This role oversees end-to-end infrastructure projects within a system integration environment, including network, server, cloud, and security solutions, ensuring high performance, scalability, and reliability for both internal operations and external clients. The position involves managing the full project lifecycle — from requirement gathering and solution design to deployment, optimization, and ongoing support. The successful candidate will work closely with cross-functional teams, including sales, presales, and delivery units, to ensure solutions are aligned with client requirements and industry best practices.
 
Job Responsibilities

Lead Infrastructure Delivery: Oversee the design, implementation, and management of enterprise IT infrastructure solutions, including network, server, cloud, and security systems, ensuring high availability and performance for client environments.

Manage End-to-End Projects: Take ownership of the full project lifecycle — from requirement gathering and solution design to deployment, testing, and post-implementation support — ensuring delivery within scope, timeline, and budget.

Drive Technical Excellence: Provide hands-on technical leadership and guidance to engineering teams, ensuring adherence to best practices, architecture standards, and security compliance.

Collaborate with Stakeholders: Work closely with presales, sales, and client stakeholders to translate business needs into scalable and effective infrastructure solutions.

Optimize Operations: Monitor system performance, troubleshoot complex issues, and implement continuous improvements to enhance reliability, efficiency, and scalability of IT environments.

Support Digital Transformation: Contribute to enterprise and industrial digitalization initiatives, including cloud adoption, system modernization, and integration with smart factory or Industry 4.0 solutions.

Lead and Develop Team: Manage, mentor, and develop a team of infrastructure engineers, fostering a high-performance culture and supporting career growth and capability building.

The Partner Business Manager (PBM) is Individual Contributor or leads a team of PBMs that covers channel business for all SAP Cloud solutions (S/4 HANA RISE & S/4HANA Grow, HCM, FSM, BTP, SCM, AI, CX, etc.).  The PBM is responsible for proactively developing the partner's SAP business by helping finalize their business plan, driving sales (to net new customers and to the partner's current customer base), demand generation, build on partner capabilities to grow SAP's cloud subscription revenue across the SAP solution portfolio in designated territories. 

The PBM is the main point of contact for building the partner relationship with SAP. The PBM is the central advisor to the partner, owning the partner relationship end to end, and responsible for coordinating all SAP interactions (with executives, solutions teams, peers, etc.) to drive partner investments and growth in SAP's solutions portfolio. The PBM is responsible for appropriately managing/balancing the use of SAP assets (i.e. Pre-Sales, Training, PSA, etc.) without under- or over-utilizing those assets. He or She develops partners for long term engagements, maintaining health-of-business and transformation to new solutions, technologies and models. The Partner Business Manager supports partner enablement to drive partner self-sufficiency, from demand generation to closing deals. The PBM also supports partners in sales cycle via coaching, mentoring and shadowing in customer facing sales activities. Position is measured on the following metrics - revenue, year-over-year growth, market share growth, build on partner maturity, adoption and renewals of strategic solutions (i.e. RISE, Cloud) and Corporate segment impact including scaling Partner Led Territories business with partners.

a. Explains economic trends and industry knowledge to support the partner's investment in developing their SAP business and to gain trusted advisor status

Our Systems Engineering team ensures we have the right software requirements and specifications documented for our systems and that we have the right verification and validation in place to demonstrate them. There is a strong focus in the team on working with stakeholders from across the organization to make sure that the right information is being documented in the right place. System Integrators help support modeling of complex systems to help build common understanding of current and future system design and functionality.

We are looking for individuals who know how to analyze complex systems, recognize patterns and understand the relationships between components, their inputs and outputs and the properties of each. Team members should be strong communicators who can work with cross-functional teams to ensure that complex technical concepts are understood by all.




Responsibilities

Product and Systems Knowledge: Work and collaborate with product and systems subject matter experts such as Product Managers, and Architects to gain expert knowledge of the product’s and system’s use cases, functional and non-functional behavior, system’s components interconnectivity properties and their respective input and output specifications.

System Assessment: Evaluate  systems and components’ existing technology infrastructure, integration specifications to identify integration needs and opportunities.

Design and Planning: Collaborate with Customers, Architects, Release Train Engineerings, Product Managers, development teams and other internal teams to analyze, design and plan comprehensive systems integration and verification activities. Participate in the development of system requirements and specifications for the products and systems under your purview.

Integration Test Development: Develop, configure, automate and customize continuous integration tests to ensure seamless data flow, and correct functional and non-functional behavior.

The Specialist, Forecasting & Replenishment Systems is responsible for supporting the implementation, stabilization, and ongoing operations of the company’s demand forecasting and automated replenishment platform. Track and analyze planning KPIs (Forecast Accuracy, %OOS) and recommend improvements.

About the Role

As the Application Modernization Lead, you will be the visionary driving our enterprise-wide transition from legacy systems to a modern, agile, and secure technological landscape. Reporting directly to the SVP of IT Modernization, you will define the roadmap, design target architectures, and lead the charge in reducing technical debt while enabling future growth through Cloud-native and API-driven solutions.

Key Responsibilities

1. Strategy & Roadmap Development

Define and drive the enterprise-wide Application Modernization Strategy and roadmap.

Prioritize modernization projects based on business value, technical debt, cost, and risk.

Develop modernization blueprints for transitioning legacy systems into future-ready architectures.

2. Target Architecture Design

Design and oversee the implementation of modular, Cloud-native, and API-first Target Architectures.

Establish reference architectures, design guidelines, and reusable patterns for the organization.

3. Technology Portfolio Rationalization

Analyze the current technology stack to select appropriate technologies that align with organizational goals.

Identify systems for retirement, replacement, or replatforming to reduce complexity and operational costs.

Present TCO (Total Cost of Ownership) and ROI analysis to senior management to facilitate decision-making.

4. Vendor & Partner Management

Collaborate with external vendors to design modernization solutions.

Evaluate technical proposals and maintain high-quality delivery standards from partners.

5. Leadership & Capability Building

Manage and mentor the Application Modernization team.

Build internal capabilities to reduce long-term dependency on external vendors.

Who We Are Looking For

Qualifications:

Education: Bachelor’s or Master’s Degree in Computer Engineering, Computer Science, IT, or a related field.

Experience: At least 8 years of experience in Application Architecture, Integration, or Modernization.

Leadership: Proven track record in a leadership role (Lead, Manager, or Head) within Software Development.

Background: Experience working within medium to large-scale organizations with complex system environments.

Core Technical Skills:

Architecture Expertise: Deep knowledge of transitioning from Monolithic to Microservices, Modular Architecture, and API-first design.

Modernization Strategies: Practical experience with the 6Rs (Refactor, Replatform, Rebuild, Retire, Replace, etc.).

Integration & Cloud: Proficiency in Integration Architecture (API Gateways, Middleware, Message Queues) and Cloud-native patterns (Containers, Orchestration, Observability).

DevOps: Strong understanding of CI/CD, Infrastructure as Code (IaC), and Automation.

Legacy Knowledge: Understanding of legacy systems (e.g., older Java/.NET versions, ESB) and effective migration pathways.

Full_Description:
Position Overview 

We are looking for a highly skilled and detail-oriented Squard lead (Golang) to join our backend engineering team. This position is ideal for professionals who have a strong foundation in system design, distributed architecture, and high-performance service development using Go. You will play a key role in building scalable backend systems that power mission-critical business applications. 

 Key Responsibilities 

Design, implement, and maintain scalable, reliable, and high-performance backend services using Go (Golang). 

Develop APIs and microservices that align with product requirements and architectural standards. 

Collaborate with product managers, DevOps engineers, and front-end developers to deliver seamless end-to-end solutions. 

Write clean, efficient, and testable code, adhering to industry best practices and internal coding guidelines. 

Participate in system architecture and design reviews to ensure robustness and scalability. 

Monitor application performance, troubleshoot issues, and continuously improve system reliability. 

Contribute to continuous integration and delivery (CI/CD) workflows and development process improvements. 

Mentor junior developers and provide technical leadership as needed. 

 Qualifications 

Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, or a related technical field. 

5+ years of professional experience in backend development with a strong focus on Golang. 

Solid understanding of Go runtime, goroutines, channels, and memory management. 

Proficiency in building and consuming RESTful and/or gRPC APIs. 

Experience with relational (PostgreSQL, MySQL) and non-relational (MongoDB, Redis) databases. 

Familiarity with Docker, Kubernetes, and cloud environments such as AWS, GCP, or Azure. 

Strong understanding of distributed systems, microservices architecture, and event-driven design. 

Practical experience with version control systems (Git) and Agile/Scrum methodologies.

Job Summary : Supports the development and execution of IT strategy; assisting with analysis, and coordination of strategic IT initiatives, ensuring alignment with business goals.

Key Responsibilities

Strategic Planning: Participate in IT strategy planning and execution, including the preparation of the IT roadmap.

Collaboration & Data Collection: Work with various Business Units (BUs) and IT teams to gather and analyze data to support strategic recommendations.

Performance Monitoring: Help develop and track Key Performance Indicators (KPIs) and prepare reports/dashboards to communicate IT performance to senior leadership.

Trend Analysis: Stay informed about emerging IT trends and best practices to integrate these insights into the bank’s strategy for a competitive edge.

Regulatory Alignment: Ensure all IT strategic initiatives adhere to financial services industry regulations, policies, and guidelines.

Focuses on Strategy and Market Expansion.




Qualifications & Required Competencies

The candidate is expected to meet specific proficiency levels across technical, individual, and leadership categories.

1. Technical / Functional Expertise

Data Gathering and Reporting (Extensive Experience): Must be able to oversee multiple data initiatives, analyze complex reports, and develop criteria for selecting reporting tools .

IT Industry Trends & Directions (Extensive Experience): Ability to compare the bank’s technology practices against competitors, establish benchmarks, and conceptualize products leveraging emerging tech .

Data Analysis & Visualization (Working Knowledge): Proficient in deriving actionable insights from data and using tools like Tableau or Datawrapper to tell compelling stories .

Business Growth Strategies (Working Knowledge): Capable of tracking project success under approved strategies and documenting SWOT analyses for strategic planning .

2. Business & Framework Knowledge

Scaled Agile Framework (SAFe): Requires Working Knowledge to follow SAFe principles, ensure technical requirements align with business objectives, and guide projects using lean-agile practices .

Design Thinking: Requires Working Knowledge to perform design tasks, document personas, and present prototypes to stakeholders based on feedback .

3. Leadership & Individual Competencies

Decision Making & Critical Thinking: Ability to prioritize concerns, assess risks, and organize relevant data to reach productive decisions .

Teamwork: A supportive collaborator who shares knowledge proactively and participates actively in team meetings .

Growth Mindset: Demonstrates a willingness to learn from mistakes, seeks feedback for professional growth, and tries new things .

Alignment & Results Orientation: Ability to align daily activities with business goals and take full responsibility for meeting deadlines and work outcome.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Business Administration, or related field.

Proven experience in managing SAP projects (e.g., SAP S/4HANA, SAP ECC).

Strong knowledge of SAP modules such as FI/CO, MM, SD, or PP.

Solid understanding of project management methodologies (Agile, Waterfall, or hybrid).

Excellent leadership, communication, and stakeholder management skills.

Strong problem-solving and risk management abilities.

Experience with project management tools (e.g., MS Project, Jira).

PMP, PRINCE2, or SAP certification is an advantage.

Good command of English (both written and spoken).

Preferred Skills

Experience in regional or global SAP implementation projects.

Familiarity with SAP Activate methodology.

Ability to work in a fast-paced, dynamic environment.

Job_Title: Head of PMO (Business Transformation - Government Sector)
Full_Description:
OVERVIEW

เรากำลังมองหาผู้นำทีม PMO ระดับมืออาชีพที่มีประสบการณ์สูงในธุรกิจ SI และมีความเชี่ยวชาญในการส่งมอบโปรเจกต์ Business Transformation ให้กับหน่วยงานภาครัฐ คุณจะมีบทบาทสำคัญในการวางมาตรฐานการทำงาน (Governance) บริหารจัดการทรัพยากร และควบคุมดูแลทีม Project Manager (PM), Business Analyst (BA) และ Quality Assurance (QA) เพื่อให้มั่นใจว่าโครงการระดับประเทศจะสำเร็จลุล่วงตามแผนงาน คุณภาพ และงบประมาณ

 

KEY RESPONSIBILITIES

Team Leadership: บริหารจัดการและพัฒนาศักยภาพทีม PM, BA และ QA ให้ทำงานสอดประสานกันอย่างมีประสิทธิภาพ

Project Governance: กำหนดมาตรฐาน Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs), Templates และ Methodology (Waterfall & Agile) สำหรับการส่งมอบงานระบบสารสนเทศเพื่อการให้บริการขนาดใหญ่

Government Delivery: ควบคุมการส่งมอบงานตามงวดงานในสัญญา (TOR) ของภาครัฐ ประสานงานการตรวจรับงาน และบริหารจัดการความเสี่ยง (Risk Management) ที่อาจส่งผลต่อการเบิกจ่าย

Resource Management: วางแผนการใช้ทรัพยากร (Capacity Planning) ในทุกโครงการ เพื่อลดคอขวดในขั้นตอนการเก็บ Requirement (BA) หรือการทดสอบระบบ (QA)

Stakeholder Management: เป็นตัวกลางประสานงานระหว่างทีมเทคนิค ผู้บริหารระดับสูง และคณะกรรมการตรวจรับของหน่วยงานภาครัฐ

Quality Assurance: กำหนดเกณฑ์มาตรฐานการทดสอบระบบ (UAT/SIT) และกระบวนการประกันคุณภาพเพื่อให้ระบบที่ส่งมอบมีความเสถียรและปลอดภัยตามมาตรฐานความมั่นคงปลอดภัยไซเบอร์

 

QUALIFICATIONS

Experience: มีประสบการณ์รวมไม่น้อยกว่า 10-15 ปี โดยต้องมีประสบการณ์อย่างน้อย 5 ปีในระดับบริหารจัดการทีม (PMO/Project Director) ในธุรกิจ SI

Domain Knowledge: มีประสบการณ์ตรงในการทำโครงการ Technology Transformation หรือวางระบบ Core System ให้กับ หน่วยงานภาครัฐ/รัฐวิสาหกิจ (เข้าใจระเบียบพัสดุและ e-GP เป็นอย่างดี)

Technical Savvy: เข้าใจ SDLC (Software Development Life Cycle) อย่างลึกซึ้ง และสามารถคุยภาษาเทคนิคกับทีม Developer ได้

Skills: มีทักษะการเจรจาต่อรอง (Negotiation) และการแก้ปัญหาเฉพาะหน้าภายใต้ความกดดันสูง

Certification: ได้รับใบรับรอง PMP, PRINCE2 หรือ Agile Certification จะพิจารณาเป็นพิเศษ

Language: สามารถสื่อสารได้อย่างมืออาชีพ รวมถึงการเขียนรายงาน/เอกสารโครงการ และสื่อสารภาษาอังกฤษได้ดี

Experience in business analysis or related fields.

Knowledge / Skills

Strong analytical and problem-solving skills.

Good command of English (written and spoken).

Proficiency in Microsoft Office (Excel, PowerPoint, and Word).

Key Responsibilities

Provide first-level technical support to end users both onsite and remotely.

Install, configure, and maintain IT equipment including laptops, desktops, printers, and mobile devices.

Troubleshoot issues related to operating systems, software applications, and network connectivity.

Support and maintain Microsoft 365 services such as Outlook, Teams, and SharePoint.

Manage user accounts and access permissions through systems such as Active Directory or Azure AD.

Monitor and respond to support requests using IT ticketing systems (e.g., ServiceNow, Jira, Zendesk).

Collaborate with regional and global IT teams to resolve complex technical issues.

Support IT onboarding and offboarding processes for employees.

Maintain and track IT asset inventory and ensure proper documentation.

Assist in implementing IT policies, security standards, and global IT procedures.

Qualifications

Education

Bachelor’s degree in

Information Technology

Computer Science

Computer Engineering

or a related field

1–3 years of experience in IT Support, Helpdesk, or Technical Support roles.

Experience working in a multinational or global organization is preferred.

Company: ผู้ประกอบการไม่เปิดเผยชื่อบริษัท
Full_Description:
LEIGHTON ASIA
Established in 1974 and headquartered in Hong Kong, Leighton Asia delivers a portfolio of high-profile infrastructure projects throughout Asia. We are a proven leader in the delivery of complex tunnel, rail and road networks, offering a comprehensive range of services spanning key sectors including civil infrastructure, advanced technology, buildings, energy and resources, and MEP.

Leighton Asia currently operates in Hong Kong, Macau, Singapore, Philippines, Indonesia, Malaysia, India and Thailand.

Leveraging our international expertise and local knowledge, we are committed to delivering value-added solutions, bespoke and high-quality projects, and generating sustainable returns for our clients, while providing safe, rewarding and fulfilling careers for our people. Our ability to deliver results to our clients, stakeholders and communities across a wide and culturally diverse region is made possible by our committed and high performing “One Leighton Asia Team”.

Leighton Asia is a member of the CIMIC Group, an engineering-led construction, mining, services and public private partnerships leader with a history dating back to 1899.

Key Responsibilities

Plan and coordinate the integration of ELV systems, including Building Management System (BMS), Energy Performance Monitoring System (EPMS), and structured cabling systems.

Review system requirements, points lists, and integration design in accordance with project specifications and Basis of Design.

Coordinate with consultants, contractors, vendors and internal teams to ensure smooth implementation of ELV integration works.

Oversee installation, configuration and commissioning of ELV hardware, software and networking components.

Conduct testing, validation and troubleshooting of integrated systems to ensure reliability and compliance with project standards.

Verify system performance including end-to-end testing (Point to Graphics) and functional testing of controllers, PLCs and I/O panels.

Prepare integration documentation, technical reports and system records for project management and client review.

Support training and handover activities for operation and maintenance teams.

Ensure all ELV works comply with relevant electrical safety regulations and site safety requirements.

Qualifications

Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical Engineering, Control Engineering or related discipline.

Professional certification or relevant technical qualifications will be an advantage.

Strong knowledge of ELV systems integration within data center, industrial or critical facilities environments.

Amazon Web Services (AWS) is leading the next phase of AI adoption and is seeking a hands-on AI Specialist Solution Architect (SSA). AWS Specialist Solutions Architects (SSAs) are technologists with deep domain-specific expertise, able to address advanced concepts and feature designs.

As part of the AWS sales organization, SSAs work with customers who have complex challenges that require expert-level knowledge to solve. SSAs craft scalable, flexible, and resilient technical architectures that address those challenges. This might involve guiding customers as they refactor an application or design an entirely new cloud-based system.

Specialist SAs play a critical role in capturing customer feedback, advocating for roadmap enhancements and anticipating customer requirements as they work backwards from their needs. As domain experts, SSAs also participate in field engagement and enablement, producing content such as whitepapers, blogs, and workshops for customers, partners, and the AWS Technical Field.

This role focuses on converting AI ambition into programs that can be delivered, operated, and scaled in production environments.

Key job responsibilities
- The AI Specialist SA team builds technical relationships with customers of all sizes and operate as their trusted advisor, ensuring they get the most out of the cloud at every stage of their journey while adopting GenAI/ML and Agentic technologies across their organisation.
- You’ll manage the overall technical relationship between AWS and our customers, making recommendations on security, cost, performance, reliability and operational efficiency to accelerate their challenging GenAI/ML and Agentic projects.
- Internally, you will be the voice of the customer, sharing their needs with regard to their usage of our services impacting the roadmap of AWS GenAI/ML and Agentic features.
- In this role, your creativity will link technology to tangible solutions, with the opportunity to define cloud-native GenAI/ML and Agentic architectural patterns for a variety of use cases.
- You will participate in the creation and sharing of best practices, technical content and new reference architectures (e.g. white papers, code samples, blog posts) and evangelize and educate about running GenAI/ML and Agentic workloads on AWS technology (e.g. through workshops, user groups, meetups, public speaking, online videos or conferences).
- Technical Leadership & Mentorship: Lead hands-on deep dives and technical workshops, contributing reusable code, reference architectures, and internal technical assets for the broader engineering organization.

About the team
Diverse Experiences
AWS values diverse experiences. Even if you do not meet all of the qualifications and skills listed in the job description, we encourage candidates to apply. If your career is just starting, hasn’t followed a traditional path, or includes alternative experiences, don’t let it stop you from applying.

Why AWS?
Amazon Web Services (AWS) is the world’s most comprehensive and broadly adopted cloud platform. We pioneered cloud computing and never stopped innovating — that’s why customers from the most successful startups to Global 500 companies trust our robust suite of products and services to power their businesses.

Inclusive Team Culture
Here at AWS, it’s in our nature to learn and be curious. Our employee-led affinity groups foster a culture of inclusion that empower us to be proud of our differences. Ongoing events and learning experiences, including our Conversations on Race and Ethnicity (CORE) and AmazeCon conferences, inspire us to never stop embracing our uniqueness.

Mentorship & Career Growth
We’re continuously raising our performance bar as we strive to become Earth’s Best Employer. That’s why you’ll find endless knowledge-sharing, mentorship and other career-advancing resources here to help you develop into a better-rounded professional.

Work/Life Balance
We value work-life harmony. Achieving success at work should never come at the expense of sacrifices at home, which is why we strive for flexibility as part of our working culture. When we feel supported in the workplace and at home, there’s nothing we can’t achieve in the cloud.

- 7+ year design/implementation/consulting experience of distributed applications.
- 5+ years customer facing experience in design/implementation of production AI systems.
- Experience implementing AI solutions that can include integration of LLMs/multi-modal FMs in large scale systems, fine-tuning LLMs, deployment and distributed inference of LLMs, RAG, FM evaluation, Vector DBs, Agentic workflows, prompt/context engineering, and MLOps.
- Hands-on experience with AWS ecosystems (including Bedrock, AgentCore, and SageMaker) or equivalent to set up secure, private-network AI environments, and practical experience implementing Retrieval-Augmented Generation using embeddings, vector stores, and semantic search optimization.
- Able to effectively communicate across an increasing diversity of audiences internally and externally.
- Ability to influence customer and internal business decision makers as a technical thought leader.
